preamble: The shift from the voiceless labiodental fricative [f] to the voiced labio-velar approximant [w] is a process of lenition and vocalization. It involves a multi-stage weakening (f → v → ʋ → w) where the sound gains voicing and loses friction, eventually stabilizing as a glide.

examples.note: Intervocalic lenition of historical /f/ toward voiced fricatives or approximants.

sources: Lass, R. (1997). Historical Linguistics and Language Change.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
